revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Volume needed/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Volume needed/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Volume needed/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Volume needed/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Volume needed/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Volume needed/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

{{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23692667944|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tlx|Volume needed}} template functions as an in-line request for the specific citation of a volume and/or issue number in a serial publication such as a journal, newspaper or comic book. Do not use this tag in order to label statements which you suspect to be false. Instead, use a dispute template such as {{tlx|Dubious}}, or move those statements to the talk page and ask for evidence. By default, it asks for both volume & issue number. This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]] but not this template itself. This [[Wikipedia:Template|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Avoid self-references|self-reference]] and so is part of the Wikipedia project rather than the encyclopedic content. ===Parameters=== * {{para|date|{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}   When the tag was added. * {{para|issue|no}}   or {{para|iss|no}}   or {{para|i|no}}   Used in the unusual case that an issue is specified but the volume is missing. Note that some publication do not use issue numbers, though this is uncommon other than for book series. Any value will work, e.g. {{para|issue|false}} or {{para|i|0}}. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|issue=no}} * {{para|volume|no}}   or {{para|vol|no}}   or {{para|v|no}}   Used in the unusual case that a volume is specified but the issue is missing. Note that some publication do not have a volume number, though this is uncommon. Any value will work. Result: {{Volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}|volume=no}} ===Journal and news usage=== (In this example it is essentially flagging the fact that volume, issue number ''and'' month of publication are all missing, by including them as empty parameters that need filling; i.e., this example assumes that the original editor's citation omitted all of this information. Because of peculiarities of the {{tl|cite journal}} and {{tl|cite news}} templates, {{tlx|volume needed}} should go in those templates' |issue= field rather than |volume=, or it will be boldfaced. Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{volume needed|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{Reflist}} '''Produces:''' Some text here.{{Cite journal |last=Smith |first=Jane |title=The Unbearable Beingness of Light |journal=Proceedings of the XYZ |issue={{fix |link=Wikipedia:Citing sources |title=This citation requires a reference to the specific volume in which the source appears. |text=volume & issue needed}} |volume= |year=2009 |pages=47–62 |location=[[New York City|New York]] |publisher=Fnord Pubs }} ... {{reflist}} ===Comic book usage=== Usage with {{tl|Cite comic}} is the same as above, except {{tlx|volume needed}} '''must''' go in the |volume= field, or it will not be displayed, and the |c=y parameter & value must be added, so that flagged articles are categorized under [[:Category:Comics articles needing issue citations]] instead of the more general [[:Category:Articles with unsourced statements]]: {{tlx|volume needed|c{{=}}y|{{subst:DATE}}}} ==Redirects== *{{tl|issue needed}} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] *{{tl|Citation needed}} for other source citation cleanup templates *{{tl|Page needed}} (similar to {{tl|volume needed}}, for missing page numbers) *{{tl|Season needed}} (similar, for missing TV season and episode number) *{{tl|Time needed}} (similar, for missing time references in audio-visual resources) {{inline cleanup tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Inline citation cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
